description: Build Azure AI-powered Multilingual Financial Translator - A feature that uses Azure Translator to translate financial documents, calculator results, and advice into 10+ Indian languages (Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, Gujarati, Kannada, Malayalam, Punjabi, Odia). Free users get basic translation of calculator results (limited to 2 languages). PRO users get unlimited language access, document translation, and financial terminology glossary. This solves the massive pain point of financial literacy being locked behind English comprehension in India where 80%+ people prefer regional languages.
steps: Create the API route at app/api/azure/translate/route.ts that: (1) accepts text content and target language code, (2) calls Azure Translator using AZURE_AI_ENDPOINT and AZURE_AI_KEY environment variables with region centralindia, (3) supports batch translation of multiple text segments, (4) includes financial terminology dictionary hints for accurate domain-specific translation, (5) gates unlimited translation behind Pro (free: 2 languages - Hindi and Tamil only, max 500 chars; Pro: all languages, unlimited length, batch support). Use proper headers: Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key, Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Region, Content-Type.

Create the API route at app/api/azure/detect-language/route.ts that calls Azure Translator's language detection endpoint (https://tlate.cognitiveservices.azure.com/translator/text/v3.0/detect) to auto-detect the language of pasted text (useful for users who paste financial documents in regional languages and want English translation).

Create the client component at components/features/FinancialTranslator.tsx that provides: (1) source text input area (or paste calculator results), (2) language selector dropdown with all supported Indian languages plus flags/icons, (3) auto-detect source language button, (4) translated output with copy button, (5) financial glossary section showing key terms translated (EMI, SIP, TDS, GST, etc.), (6) Pro gate: show lock icon on premium languages and character limit warning for free users. Style following existing component patterns with Card, CardContent from ui/card.

Create the tool page at app/tools/translator/page.tsx with proper metadata targeting 'financial calculator in Hindi', 'EMI calculator Tamil', etc. Include BreadcrumbSchema and AdContainer. Add a section explaining why financial literacy in regional languages matters.

Update app/tools/page.tsx to add the Financial Translator to the TOOLS array with icon Languages (from lucide-react), color 'orange', description highlighting 10+ Indian language support, and badge 'Free + PRO'.

Create a reusable TranslateButton component at components/features/TranslateButton.tsx - a small floating button that can be placed on any calculator page to translate results. It opens a modal/popover with language selection and shows translated text. This makes translation discoverable across the entire site.
acceptance_criteria: API route at /api/azure/translate correctly calls Azure Translator and returns translated text
API route at /api/azure/detect-language correctly detects input language
Financial Translator component renders with language selection, text input/output, and glossary
Free users limited to Hindi/Tamil and 500 chars, Pro users get full access
TranslateButton component is reusable and can be placed on calculator pages
Tool page has proper SEO metadata with regional language keywords
findings: Build passes cleanly. Lint has pre-existing errors (411 errors across other files) but all FEAT-002 files pass lint with 0 errors. Used same API key and pattern as FEAT-001. TranslateButton component is a standalone floating button that accepts text prop.
